chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: 100 days of code is a lot of fun  I know that  In today's video we will learn about tuple  The list we read was very useful  We saw many methods of tuple

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: And we also saw  What we can do with this list  There is another data type in python  Which is tuple  And it is equally useful

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: Because you can't change tuple  And it has some important methods  Which I am going to tell you about  Let's go to the computer screen  And let's get started

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: The way we saw about list  Tuple is also very similar  But the difference is  You can't change tuple  And what I will do to make tuple

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: I will do something like this  I will write tuple  And I will use these brackets  And I will write values  1, 5, 6

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: If I print type  I will say type of tuple  And if I print tuple  I will get these values  3 values

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: And I will get last tuple  If there were 2 elements  Like this  I would get 1 and 5  What about if there was only 1 element

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: Then there will be a confusion  And see  Class int  Now you will say I made tuple  Of 1 element

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: So this is the biggest confusion of python developers  If you are making tuple of 1 element  Then comma is necessary  Otherwise python will get confused  That you are writing 1 value

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: In brackets  Or you are making a new tuple  Python will get confused  So you have to write 1 comma  Run it like this

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: So class tuple is made  Of 1 element  Tuples are ordered collection of data items  They store multiple items in a single variable  Tuple items are separated by commas

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: And enclosed within round brackets  Tuples are unchangeable  Means you cannot change tuples  If there was a list  Then I could have done something like this

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: That  Tuple 0 is equal to  90  And then the 0th value of tuple would have changed  But here you see

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: Type error  Tuple object does not support item assignment  If I would have added more values  And run it like this  Then you see

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: Same error is coming  But how about if this was a list  I have written tuple  But this is a list  Type of tuple

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: Will show a list  So the first value of this has changed  So I can change in list  Now you will say  Why should I use tuple

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: I can change everything  No, it does not work like this  What happens is  Sometimes you will want to intentionally  Like you want to make a constant

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: Kind of a constant list  Which you want to change by mistake  And you are working on a very complex program  So you will want  That no one should change it by mistake

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: So you will use tuple  Now here I have given an example  I have printed both  Tuple has the same thing  If I give a value

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: Like a string  Then the string will also come here  So you can see here  It is printing like this  I hope you are understanding

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: And the rest  Almost everything is the same  Except the fact that you cannot change it  You can put some other values  Of other data type

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: That will also work  But you can't change it  Here  Now if I  Print tup0

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: Let's say I print tup0  And after this  I print tup1, tup2  I print tup34  If I print 34 then there will be an error

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: Because 34 does not exist  So see tuple index is out of range  But the program will run up to here  And you will see 1, 2, 76  Which is present on 0, 1st and 2nd

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: I hope you understood  So this way we can  Index tuple  Positive indexing and negative indexing  Are same in tuple

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: Like we talked about list  So if someone  Wrote tuple-1  Then you will not process minus 1  You will see the length

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: 3, 2, 5, 5-1, 4  So tuple is 4  0, 1, 2, 3, 4  So it is 32  In fact I printed the length wrong

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: I am sorry  Let's print it  Print the length  Double click  L-E-N

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: Let's print the length  Length is  7, so 7-1 is 6  So 0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6  So it is true print

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: So we can resolve negative indexing  Like we saw  In list  Now you can check  If

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: 342  In tuple  Yes 342  Is present  In this tuple

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: So you can do like this  Now run it  And your work will be done  So 342 is present in this tuple  You got it printed

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: If I print 3421  Then it will not be printed  It will be evaluated in false  So like this item can be checked  If it is present or not

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: Similarly like we talked about slicing  In list  We can talk about slicing here also  Slicing can be done  But you will say slicing can be done

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: But tuple is not like that  After slicing a new tuple is written  So tuple does not change  A new tuple is made  Like if I write here

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: Tube2 is equal to tube  So I will write here from 1 to 4  So a new tuple will be made  So if I print tube2  So a new tuple will be made

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: And it will be returned  Which will start from 2 and will run till 342  0, 1, 2, 3  This is minus 1 index  So 2, 76 and 342

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: It will be exactly like we talked about  Slicing of list  Similarly we can do tuple slicing  Similarly  Like we printed list

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: We can do it  It is very similar  We can convert negative indexes into positive indexes  Whenever you get to see something like this  So where there is blank

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: You can put 0  If there is blank here then you can put length of this tuple  Like I told you in list  If someone writes something like this  Tube0 to blank

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: So instead of blank  You can write length of tuple  If someone writes like this  This to 4 or this to 3  Like this

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: So you can put 0 here  If there is blank here then you can put length of this tuple  I hope this is clear  It is exactly like we did in list  So I hope you understood

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: I want you to try all the methods  Try all the methods  Try to revision list  With the help of tuple  How you will do it

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: Do slicing, it will be revisioned  As it is in list, it is in tuple  Then use in keyword  The difference is  You can't change tuple

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: You can't change its value  You can't change its length  Once you have made tuple then you will make new tuple  Or you can't change existing tuple  Tuples are immutable

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: Strings are immutable  Lists are immutable  Remember this  If you haven't accessed the playlist yet  So do access it

chunks.title: Tuples in Python
chunks.text: That's all for now  Thank you so much for watching this video
